name: Turmeric Root Extract
historicalUse: Turmeric has been in continuous medicinal use for over 4,000 years across Indian, Chinese, and Arabic civilizations. Ibn Sina described kurkum as a wound healer and liver tonic. Rambam referenced it in his pharmaceutical writings for hepatic and digestive support. In Ayurvedic tradition it is considered rasayana — a rejuvenating agent for longevity.
modernEvidence: Curcuminoids, primarily curcumin, demethoxycurcumin, and bisdemethoxycurcumin, are among the most extensively researched phytochemicals in nutritional science with over 12,000 published studies. Key actions include NF-kB inhibition, Nrf2 activation, and modulation of inflammatory eicosanoid pathways. Bioavailability is significantly enhanced by piperine co-administration.
mechanisms: NF-kB pathway inhibition reducing pro-inflammatory cytokine transcription
Nrf2 pathway activation upregulating endogenous antioxidant enzymes (HO-1, NQO1)
COX-2 and 5-LOX inhibition reducing prostaglandin and leukotriene synthesis
STAT3 signaling modulation supporting cellular homeostasis
benefits: Supports healthy inflammatory balance at the cellular level
safetyProfile: Excellent safety profile. GRAS status. Well tolerated at up to 8g/day in clinical studies. Mild GI discomfort at very high doses.
contraindications: Bile duct obstruction — curcumin stimulates bile secretion
Caution pre-surgery due to antiplatelet properties at high doses
drugInteractions: Anticoagulants — additive antiplatelet effect at high doses; monitor INR
Chemotherapy agents — consult oncologist; curcumin may modulate efficacy of certain agents
Talinolol and P-glycoprotein substrates — piperine combination may increase absorption
physicianNotes: Clinically appropriate across broad patient populations. In oncology supportive care contexts, consult treating oncologist regarding timing relative to cytotoxic agents. Bioavailability is formulation-dependent — standardized 95% curcuminoid extract with piperine provides superior bioavailability over raw turmeric.
patientGuidance: Take with a fat-containing meal for optimal absorption. If you are on blood thinners or undergoing cancer treatment, inform your physician before use.
